name: Parque Natural de las Bardenas Reales – vuelta completa
region_intro: Mountain biking in Bardenas Reales offers diverse terrain across its distinctive semi-desert landscapes. The region is characterized by clay, chalk, and sandstone formations, sculpted by erosion into canyons, plateaus, and badlands. Riders can explore extensive networks of trails that wind through these unique geological features, including iconic landmarks like Castildetierra. The varied topography provides a captivating backdrop for mountain biking adventures. Best mountain bike trails in Bardenas Reales The most popular mountain bike trail is Parque Natural de las Bardenas Reales – vuelta completa, a 19.7 miles (31.8 km) trail that takes 2 hours 53 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers a comprehensive tour of the natural park's varied terrain. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Balcón de Pilatos y Mirador Punta del Alba – circular por el Parque Natural de las Bardenas Reales, a moderate 30.4 miles (49.0 km) path. This route features panoramic views from viewpoints like Balcón de Pilatos. Local mountain bikers also love the Castildetierra – Castildetierra Hill loop from Corral de Juan Gurria, a 19.1 miles (30.8 km) trail leading through eroded semi-desert landscapes, often completed in about 2 hours 13 minutes. Mountain biking in Bardenas Reales is defined by dramatic canyons, vast plateaus, and unique badlands formations. The network offers options for various ability levels, from moderate paths to more challenging routes. The routes in Bardenas Reales are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 150 reviews. More than 900 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Bardenas Reales's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in Bardenas Reales?
A: Bardenas Reales offers an extensive network of over 700 kilometers of tracks and paths. On komoot, you can find more than 30 mountain bike routes, catering to various skill levels, from easy to difficult.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when mountain biking in Bardenas Reales?
A: The terrain in Bardenas Reales is unique, characterized by its semi-desert landscapes. You'll encounter clay, chalk, and sandstone formations sculpted by erosion into dramatic canyons, plateaus, and badlands. While some areas have limited elevation gain, providing accessible rides, others offer undulating paths and rapid descents for a more engaging experience.

Q: Are there any iconic landmarks or viewpoints to see along the mountain bike trails?
A: Yes, Bardenas Reales is home to several iconic natural features. You can ride past the famous &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/945742" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castildetierra&lt;/a&gt;, an isolated hill resembling a clay pyramid. Other notable formations include Piskerra. For panoramic views, consider routes that pass by &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3927882" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bardena Blanca Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e988504077/balcon-de-pilatos-y-mirador-punta-del-alba-circular-por-el-parque-natural-de-las-bardenas-reales" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Balcón de Pilatos&lt;/a&gt;, which offer spectacular vistas of the park's vastness.

Q: What is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Bardenas Reales?
A: The best time for mountain biking in Bardenas Reales is between September and June. During these months, temperatures are more pleasant. It's advisable to avoid the extreme heat of summer (July and August) and be aware that heavy rain can lead to muddy conditions, especially on the clay-rich trails.

Q: Are there family-friendly mountain bike trails in Bardenas Reales?
A: Yes, Bardenas Reales offers routes suitable for families. Many areas have limited elevation gain, making them accessible for a wide range of cyclists. There are 11 easy routes available on komoot, providing gentler options for a family outing.

Q: Do I need a permit to mountain bike in Bardenas Reales?
A: Generally, mountain biking is permitted on designated tracks and paths within Bardenas Reales. However, it's always recommended to check the latest regulations from the official Bardenas Reales Natural Park authorities, as rules can change. You can find more information on the official tourism website for Navarra: &lt;a href="https://www.visitnavarra.es/en/-/what-to-see-in-and-around-bardenas-reales" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;visitnavarra.es&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I find circular mountain bike routes in Bardenas Reales?
A: Yes, many mountain bike routes in Bardenas Reales are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38266538" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castildetierra – Castildetierra Hill loop from Corral de Juan Gurria&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate circular trail that takes you through the eroded semi-desert landscapes.

Q: Where can I park when visiting Bardenas Reales for mountain biking?
A: Parking is generally available at key access points and visitor centers within or near the Bardenas Reales Natural Park. Specific parking details often depend on the starting point of your chosen route. It's advisable to check route descriptions for recommended parking areas.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Bardenas Reales?
A: The mountain bike routes in Bardenas Reales are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 150 reviews. Riders often praise the unique 'lunar' landscapes, the stunning scenery that changes with the light, and the extensive network of trails that cater to various abilities.

Q: Are there options for advanced mountain bikers seeking a challenge?
A: Yes, Bardenas Reales offers challenging routes for experienced mountain bikers. While many areas have limited elevation gain, the varied topography includes undulating paths and rapid descents. There are 4 difficult routes listed on komoot, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e988496651/parque-natural-de-las-bardenas-reales-vuelta-completa" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parque Natural de las Bardenas Reales – vuelta completa&lt;/a&gt;, which provides a comprehensive tour of the park's varied and demanding terrain.

Q: What kind of wildlife might I spot while mountain biking in Bardenas Reales?
A: Bardenas Reales is a UNESCO Biosphere Reserve, known for its diverse birdlife. If you're lucky, especially around viewpoints like Balcón de Pilatos, you might spot griffon vultures, golden eagles, and Egyptian vultures soaring overhead. The Bardena Negra, the greenest part of the park, also supports various plant and animal species.

Q: Are there places to eat or get refreshments near the trails?
A: While the Bardenas Reales itself is a natural park, nearby villages such as Arguedas offer amenities including cafes and restaurants where you can refuel before or after your ride. It's always a good idea to carry sufficient water and snacks, especially when venturing into the more remote parts of the park.

Q: Is public transport available to Bardenas Reales?
A: Direct public transport options into the heart of Bardenas Reales are limited. Most visitors access the park by car or through organized tours. Some nearby towns may have bus connections, but for flexibility and access to trailheads, a private vehicle is generally recommended.
tips:
It is not in vain that this landscape was declared a biosphere reserve by UNESCO. Exciting rock formations, a lunar landscape, gorges, plateaus and breathtaking views await you in the semi-desert. It's no surprise that many major movies have been filmed here, including "Game of Thrones" and "James Bond." Although the area is protected, it is well developed for visitors. Of course, the best way to explore the landscape here is on a mountain bike.

Please note the opening hours of the natural park: from 8 in the morning until one hour before sunset.
This is another of the natural viewpoints of the Bardenas that are well worth a visit. It helps you get an idea of the scale of the huge and magical place you are in.

The Bardenas Reales park is full of rock and earth formations with the shapes you can imagine and all of them very striking. One of the most representative along with the heads is La Ralla, which is distinguished by its vertical "nerves" and horizontal cutting lines caused by thousands of years of erosion.
One of the most impressive pieces of earth.
